Abandoned

Since using dir entry pointer to resolve hash conflict will involve complicate lock issue, I will instead using same_hash_offset to solve hash conflict issue. i.e. merge 9699 and 10109 to 9191.

parents.subject: LU-4603 nfs: fix offset problems of readdir

commit.subject: LU-4715 lmv: skip . and .. for slave stripe
commit.message:
LU-4715 lmv: skip . and .. for slave stripe

1. skip . and .. for slave stripe.
2. If user buffer is full in ll_dir_read, remember
the last unsuccessful entry in op_hash_offset, so in next
read, it will locate entry by >= hash_offset,  instead
of >.

Signed-off-by: wang di <di.wang@intel.com>
Change-Id: I36f17ae8955053a95b0f45714c5ff756931ce4fa
